name: ExtensionChargeItem_Product
title: R5: product additional types
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `ChargeItem.product` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`ChargeItem.product` 0..* `CodeableReference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Device,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Medication,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Substance)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `ChargeItem.product` 0..* `CodeableReference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Device,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Medication,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Substance)`
*  R4B: `ChargeItem.product[x]` 0..1 `CodeableConcept, Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Device,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Medication,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Substance)`
*  R4: `ChargeItem.product[x]` 0..1 `CodeableConcept, Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Device,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Medication,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Substance)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `ChargeItem.product` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `ChargeItem.product` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `ChargeItem.product[x]` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `ChargeItem.product` do not cover the following types: CodeableReference.
The target context `ChargeItem.product[x]` is a choice-type element and cannot directly hold extensions. The context is moved up to parent element `ChargeItem`.

element.short: DataType slice for a FHIR R5 `CodeableReference` value
element.definition: Slice to indicate the presence of a R5 `CodeableReference` in FHIR R4

element.short: R5: Reference to a concept (by class) (new)
element.comment:
Element `CodeableReference.concept` is part of an existing definition because parent element `CodeableReference` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `CodeableReference.concept` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `CodeableReference` is not mapped.
Element `CodeableReference.concept` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.definition: A reference to a concept - e.g. the information is identified by its general class to the degree of precision found in the terminology.
